macro_segments.title: Rabbinic Enactments Protecting Property and the Land: Small Animals, Chickens, Pigs, Dogs, and Pigeon Traps

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna on Takkanot Meruba: Prohibitions on raising small animals in Eretz Yisrael
Prohibition on raising chickens in Jerusalem and by kohanim due to concerns of tumah
Absolute prohibition on raising pigs in any location
Requirement to chain dogs; concerns about biting and frightening pregnant women
Prohibition on laying pigeon traps within thirty ris of populated areas

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Small Animals: Permitted in Forests and Syria; Distinction between Small and Large Animals

micro_segments.title: Baraita distinguishing small animals in Eretz Yisrael forests versus Syria; permitted raising of large animals
Rationale: small animals can be imported, large animals cannot; therefore large animal raising is permitted
Permission to purchase small animals for Yom Tov up to thirty days before, but not longer
Gemara clarifying the thirty-day limit and explanation of the prohibition after Yom Tov
Story of Rabban Gamliel permitting small animals for personal use when purchased and tied, not grazed

macro_segments.title: Narrative of the Righteous Person and the Goat: Medical Need Versus Community Standards

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Story of a righteous person prescribed to drink fresh goat milk, procuring and keeping a goat tied to bed
Visitors' reaction to the tied goat as evidence of armed robbery in the house; righteous person's deathbed confession

macro_segments.title: Story of Rabbi Yishmael's Family and the Danger of Lax Grazing Practices in Forests

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yishmael's account of his family's wealth destroyed due to grazing in forests and private judgment of monetary cases
Explanation of the transgression: crossing through small fields causing crop damage

macro_segments.title: Leniencies for Repentant Shepherds, Non-Jewish Inheritors, and Vow-Makers in Eretz Yisrael

micro_segments.title: Shepherd doing teshuva: gradual sale requirement rather than immediate liquidation
Ger inheriting dogs or pigs: gradual sale permitted to avoid financial ruin
Vow to purchase house or marry woman in Eretz Yisrael: no compulsion to fulfill immediately, wait for suitable option
Story of a woman taking absolute vow not to refuse any suitor; sages interpret as conditional on suitability

macro_segments.title: Wild and Domestic Small Animals: Permitted Types and the Case of Cats

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar permits village dogs, cats, monkeys, and genets for keeping houses clean
Definition and purpose of genets: small animals that catch mice and keep houses clean
Identification of khuldot sna'im as creatures with short legs that graze among thorn bushes

macro_segments.title: Extension of Small Animal Prohibition to Babylonia; Story of Rav Huna's Curse

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda in name of Rav: treating Babylonia like Eretz Yisrael regarding small animal prohibition
Rav Adda bar Ahava challenging Rav Huna about keeping sheep; curse that Rav Huna's children did not survive

macro_segments.title: The Incident of the Dangerous Cat and Ruling on Cats

micro_segments.title: Story of Rav, Shmuel, and Rav Assi: hierarchy and deference at a bris/pidyon haben ceremony
White cat attacks child during the ceremony; Rav rules cats are permitted to kill and forbidden to keep
Gemara questions: if killing is permitted, why must ownership be forbidden? And why no obligation to return dead cats?
Challenge from baraita: Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar permits cats for cleaning; resolution via distinction between black and white cats
Explanation: white cats and their offspring are dangerous; black cats are permitted for pest control

macro_segments.title: Miscellaneous Rulings on Opportunity and Community Welfare

micro_segments.title: Teaching on open boils and calling out even on Shabbat; closed doors and seizing opportunity
Various explanations of 'a closed door does not quickly open': refers to smicha and lost opportunities

macro_segments.title: Permitting Writing Deeds on Shabbat for House Purchase in Eretz Yisrael

micro_segments.title: Halacha: one buying house in Eretz Yisrael may tell a non-Jew to write deed even on Shabbat
Rationale: to settle Eretz Yisrael, rabbis permitted amira le-nochri despite being rabbinic violation
Broader principle: amira le-nochri permitted for mitzvot; ישוב ארץ ישראל creates exception to usual rules

macro_segments.title: Compulsory Purchase of Pathways for Town Ownership in Eretz Yisrael

micro_segments.title: One purchasing a town in Eretz Yisrael must be compelled to purchase pathways in all four directions

topical_tags.term: behema dakka (small animals - sheep and goats)
Eretz Yisrael - protection of land and settlement
Yom Tov - purchasing animals before holidays
Rabban Gamliel - ruling on purchasing and keeping animals
grazing (re'ia) - damage to crops and land
cats - white vs black, dangerous vs safe
Rav Yehuda and Rav - extending prohibitions to Babylonia
Rav Huna - curse story regarding animals
yishuv Eretz Yisrael (settlement of Land of Israel)
